prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `54dc2872`.
codex_response:
The rule is: each multicolor corner-L object has a single accent color; if there is an isolated singleton of that accent color elsewhere, the whole object is translated so its occupied elbow corner lands on that singleton. Unmatched L-objects stay put, matched singletons disappear, and unmatched singletons remain.

I discarded the summary hint and documented it in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_54dc2872/notes.md), because it said the accent cell lands on the marker. The official examples show that the elbow corner lands on the marker instead. No new shared primitive was needed.

Validation passed: `3/3` official examples, `100/100` spot-generated examples, and `1000/1000` built examples via `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 54dc2872`. I also checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/54dc2872/previews/preview_01.png) visually; the generated family looks on-distribution for the official task.
